description: Brillouin zone (BZ) Fourier decomposition of scalar curvature for the 2D Ising model at J_c
purpose: Demonstrates that d_R = 10/9 is a collective BZ property, not dominated by a single mode

collectivity_summary.interpretation: frac_kmin declines as L^{-(d_R-1)} = L^{-1/9} while |R_kmin| saturates to ~57-59. The curvature scaling is collective: all BZ modes contribute O(n) each, with the anomalous n^{1/9} excess distributed across the full Brillouin zone.

ricci_identity_check.description: T1/T3 ratio should be exactly -2.0 (Ricci decomposition identity R_1 + 2*R_3 = 0)

notes.method: Exact transfer matrix + BLAS-batched S3 + Parseval FFT decomposition
notes.key_insight: The k_min mode contributes O(1) curvature while generic modes contribute O(n) each. The anomalous exponent d_R - 1 = 1/9 is distributed collectively across all ~n BZ modes, each contributing O(n^{1/9}) excess.

papers: Paper #10: Scaling Closure Theorem, Section 5 (DBC mechanism)
